Package io.foldright.inspectablewrappers

Class Inspector

java.lang.Object
io.foldright.inspectablewrappers.Inspector
@ParametersAreNonnullByDefault public final class Inspector extends Object
This Inspector class is used to inspect the wrapper chain.

Common simple usages

  • Reports whether any instance on the wrapper chain matches the given type by static method isInstanceOf(Object, Class)
  • Retrieve the attachment from wrapper chain(wrapper instances implement interface Wrapper) by static method getAttachment(Object, Object)

Advanced usages

  • Reports whether any instance on the wrapper chain satisfy the given Predicate by static method inspect(Object, Predicate)
  • Traverses the wrapper chain, and applies the given Function to each instance on the wrapper chain, by static method travel(Object, Function)

You can implement your own inspection logic using above advanced methods.

Author:
Jerry Lee (oldratlee at gmail dot com), Zava (zava dot kid at gmail dot com)
See Also:
  • Wrapper
  • Attachable
  • WrapperAdapter

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static <W, K, V> V
    getAttachment(W wrapper, K key)
    Retrieves the attachment of instance on the wrapper chain for the given key by calling Attachable.getAttachment(Object).
    static <W> boolean
    inspect(W wrapper, Predicate<? super W> predicate)
    Reports whether any instance on the wrapper chain satisfy the given predicate.
    static <W> boolean
    isInstanceOf(W wrapper, Class<?> clazz)
    Reports whether any instance on the wrapper chain matches the given type.
    static <W, T> Optional<T>
    travel(W wrapper, Function<? super W,Optional<T>> process)
    Traverses the wrapper chain, and applies the given process function to each instance on the wrapper chain, and returns the first non-empty(Optional.empty()) result of the process function, otherwise returns Optional.empty().

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • isInstanceOf

      public static <W> boolean isInstanceOf(W wrapper, Class<?> clazz)
      Reports whether any instance on the wrapper chain matches the given type.

      The wrapper chain consists of wrapper itself, followed by the wrappers obtained by repeatedly calling Wrapper.unwrap().

      Type Parameters:
      W - the type of instances that be wrapped
      Parameters:
      wrapper - wrapper instance/wrapper chain
      clazz - target type
      Returns:
      return false if no wrapper on the wrapper chain matches the given type, otherwise return true
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any arguments is null or any wrapper {Wrapper.unwrap()} returns null
      IllegalStateException - if the adaptee of WrapperAdapter is type Wrapper
      See Also:
      • WrapperAdapter.adaptee()

    • getAttachment

      @Nullable public static <W, K, V> V getAttachment(W wrapper, K key)
      Retrieves the attachment of instance on the wrapper chain for the given key by calling Attachable.getAttachment(Object).

      The wrapper chain consists of wrapper itself, followed by the wrappers obtained by repeatedly calling Wrapper.unwrap().

      If the key exists in multiple wrappers, outer wrapper win.

      Type Parameters:
      W - the type of instances that be wrapped
      K - the type of attachment key
      V - the type of attachment value
      Parameters:
      wrapper - wrapper instance
      key - the attachment key
      Returns:
      the attachment value of wrapper of given key on the wrapper chain, or null if the attachment is absent
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any arguments is null or any wrapper {Wrapper.unwrap()} returns null
      ClassCastException - if the return value is not type <V>
      IllegalStateException - if the adaptee of WrapperAdapter is type Wrapper
      See Also:
      • Attachable.getAttachment(Object)

    • inspect

      public static <W> boolean inspect(W wrapper, Predicate<? super W> predicate)
      Reports whether any instance on the wrapper chain satisfy the given predicate.

      The wrapper chain consists of wrapper itself, followed by the wrappers obtained by repeatedly calling Wrapper.unwrap().

      Type Parameters:
      W - the type of instances that be wrapped
      Parameters:
      wrapper - wrapper instance/wrapper chain
      predicate - inspect logic
      Returns:
      return false if no wrapper on the wrapper chain satisfy the given predicate, otherwise return true
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any arguments is null or any wrapper {Wrapper.unwrap()} returns null
      IllegalStateException - if the adaptee of WrapperAdapter is type Wrapper

    • travel

      @NonNull public static <W, T> Optional<T> travel(W wrapper, Function<? super W,Optional<T>> process)
      Traverses the wrapper chain, and applies the given process function to each instance on the wrapper chain, and returns the first non-empty(Optional.empty()) result of the process function, otherwise returns Optional.empty().

      The wrapper chain consists of wrapper itself, followed by the wrappers obtained by repeatedly calling Wrapper.unwrap().

      Type Parameters:
      W - the type of instances that be wrapped
      T - the return data type of process function
      Parameters:
      wrapper - wrapper instance
      process - process function
      Returns:
      the first non-empty(Optional.empty()) result of the process function, otherwise returns Optional.empty().
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if any arguments is null or any wrapper {Wrapper.unwrap()} returns null
      IllegalStateException - if the adaptee of WrapperAdapter is type Wrapper
